segments.text: Even today my complaint is bitter; his hand is heavy in spite of my groaning.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If only I knew where to find him; if only I could go to his dwelling!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would state my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would find out what he would answer me, and consider what he would say to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Would he vigorously oppose me? No, he would not press charges against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There the upright can establish their innocence before him, and there I would be delivered forever from my judge.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But if I go to the east, he is not there; if I go to the west, I do not find him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When he is at work in the north, I do not see him; when he turns to the south, I catch no glimpse of him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My feet have closely followed his steps; I have kept to his way without turning aside.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have not departed from the commands of his lips; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my daily bread.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But he stands alone, and who can oppose him? He does whatever he pleases.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He carries out his decree against me, and many such plans he still has in store.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: That is why I am terrified before him; when I think of all this, I fear him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet I am not silenced by the darkness, by the thick darkness that covers my face.
segments.speaker: Job
